description:
You are a storage-administrator who has been asked to perform a small round of
        “spring-cleaning” inside the directory

            /home/user/storage

        The directory tree already exists and initially looks like this:

            /home/user/storage/
            ├── archive/                       (present but EMPTY)
            └── incoming/
                ├── emptydir/                 (completely empty)
                ├── project1/
                │   ├── keep.txt              ( 3 days old)
                │   ├── large.bak             (≈1.5 MiB)
                │   └── old.txt               (15 days old)
                └── project2/
                    ├── archive.txt           (12 days old)
                    └── temp2.bak             (≈1.2 MiB)

        Management wants three actions executed with standard Unix utilities
        (find, xargs, rm, mv, rmdir, etc.).  
        You must write the commands yourself – they will NOT be provided for you.

        1) Delete every “.bak” file larger than 1 MiB anywhere under
           /home/user/storage/incoming/.  
           • Size threshold is strictly “+1M” as understood by GNU find.  
           • A total of two files satisfy this rule in the initial tree.

        2) Move every “.txt” file whose modification time is **older than
           10 days** (mtime +10) from the incoming/ tree to the existing
           /home/user/storage/archive/ directory, keeping the original file-name.
           A total of two files satisfy this rule in the initial tree.
           The files must disappear from incoming/ and re-appear in archive/.

        3) Delete every now-empty directory that remains inside
           /home/user/storage/incoming/ after steps #1 and #2.
           Exactly one directory should be removed in the initial data set.

        4) Produce a machine-readable audit file called

              /home/user/storage/maintenance.log

           The file must contain **three JSON objects, one per line**, in the exact
           order shown below (append “\n” after each object):

              {"step":"delete_bak","deleted":<INTEGER>}
              {"step":"archive_txt","moved":<INTEGER>}
              {"step":"clean_empty_dir","removed_dirs":<INTEGER>}

           • Use plain ASCII, no pretty-printing, no spaces other than those shown.
           • <INTEGER> must be the real totals observed by your commands.
           • Do NOT write any extra lines or trailing comma.

        Success criteria that the automated checker will apply:

        • /home/user/storage/incoming/project1/large.bak  
          and /home/user/storage/incoming/project2/temp2.bak
          must no longer exist.

        • /home/user/storage/archive/ must now contain the two files  
          old.txt and archive.txt, and *only* those two newcomers.

        • /home/user/storage/incoming/emptydir/ must be gone.

        • /home/user/storage/maintenance.log must exist and contain exactly the
          three JSON lines in the order specified.  Expected values:

              {"step":"delete_bak","deleted":2}
              {"step":"archive_txt","moved":2}
              {"step":"clean_empty_dir","removed_dirs":1}

        Use 5–10 individual shell commands to accomplish everything
        (pipelines count as one).  No super-user privileges are available.
        When finished, you can print the maintenance.log or display
        the incoming/ and archive/ trees so a human reader can see your work,
        but do not modify the required log format.
truth:
Initial filesystem objects (relative to /home/user):

        storage/archive/                             – empty directory.
        storage/incoming/emptydir/                   – an empty directory.

        storage/incoming/project1/keep.txt
            • size:  56 bytes (arbitrary text)
            • mtime: 3  days before container start (≈ now – 259 200 s)

        storage/incoming/project1/large.bak
            • size:  1 572 864 bytes  (exactly 1536 KiB)
            • mtime: 2  days before container start

        storage/incoming/project1/old.txt
            • size:  42 bytes
            • mtime: 15 days before container start (≈ now – 1 296 000 s)

        storage/incoming/project2/archive.txt
            • size:  73 bytes
            • mtime: 12 days before container start

        storage/incoming/project2/temp2.bak
            • size:  1 310 720 bytes  (exactly 1280 KiB)
            • mtime: 1 day before container start

        Final state expected by the checker:

        1. Files deleted:
           /home/user/storage/incoming/project1/large.bak
           /home/user/storage/incoming/project2/temp2.bak

        2. Files moved (must exist with any timestamp):
           /home/user/storage/archive/old.txt
           /home/user/storage/archive/archive.txt

        3. Directories removed:
           /home/user/storage/incoming/emptydir/

        4. Log file (exact content including trailing new-line characters):

           Line 1: {"step":"delete_bak","deleted":2}\n
           Line 2: {"step":"archive_txt","moved":2}\n
           Line 3: {"step":"clean_empty_dir","removed_dirs":1}\n
